codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failed notification-preferences feature (commit `04025a2`) and this rerun fix (commit `4c15a48`) against the original blocking findings. The rerun resolves both blockers: runtime notification delivery is now attached once at the app shell via `MaterialApp.builder` and `RuntimeNotificationDeliverySurface` (`apps/mobile/lib/main.dart:20-23`, `apps/mobile/lib/features/settings/presentation/runtime_notification_delivery_surface.dart:20-67`), and the route-local listeners/`route.isCurrent` guards that previously stranded notifications off-thread routes were removed from thread list/detail. The cold-start leak is also closed by the new `preferencesRestored` gate in `RuntimeNotificationDeliveryState`/`RuntimeNotificationDeliveryController`, which suppresses event delivery until persisted notification preferences finish restoring (`apps/mobile/lib/features/settings/application/runtime_notification_delivery_controller.dart:59,102,129,176-196`) while `NotificationPreferencesController` flips `isLoading` only after restore completes (`apps/mobile/lib/features/settings/application/notification_preferences_controller.dart:22,75-98`). Regression coverage now explicitly exercises off-route delivery from Device settings and disabled-on-relaunch fail-closed behavior (`apps/mobile/test/features/settings/runtime_notification_delivery_surface_test.dart:18-79`, `apps/mobile/test/features/settings/runtime_notification_delivery_controller_test.dart:177-269`). No new blocking or non-blocking implementation issues were found in scope for this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/api.md` is still stale about approval targets and can mislead future workers reviewing approval-context behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/api.md:23` still says `ApprovalRecordDto.target` only carries generic identifiers like `git.branch_switch`, but bridge-core now returns the concrete destination from `PendingApprovalAction::target_name()` (`crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:580-663`) and the bridge tests assert `approval.target == "release/2026"` / security records target `release/2026` (`crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:2675-2686`).

summary: Pass. This rerun adequately fixes the two blocking issues from the prior review by moving runtime notification surfacing to an app-wide listener and fail-closing cold-start delivery until persisted notification preferences restore, with focused regression tests covering both behaviors.
